content: started using app back two thousand nineteen still called moodpath app helped understand symptom pattern making easier navigate mental health day day used consistently never came therapy doctor appointment blank mind used note app guide discussion doctor even said made working easier simple powerful tool truly support professional care
original_content: I started using this app back in 2019, when it was still called Moodpath. This app helped me understand my symptoms and patterns, making it easier to navigate my mental health day to day. Because I used it consistently, I never came to therapy or doctor appointments with a blank mind. I used the notes from the app to guide discussions, and my doctor even said it made working with me easier. A simple but powerful tool that truly supports professional care.

Highly recommended!

content: coincidentally downloaded minddoc right beginning therapy journey four year ago cool see develop grow time ive never used extra much im data person ive always consistent checkins every often take result therapy show thing changed rewarding app didnt make change happen helped notice trend pattern help make happen
original_content: I coincidentally downloaded MindDoc right before beginning my therapy journey 4 years ago. It has been very cool to see it develop and grow over time. I've never used the "extras" much. I'm a data person, so I've always been very consistent with the check-ins. Every so often, I take the results to therapy to show off how things have changed and that has been SO rewarding! This app didn't make those changes happen, but it helped me notice trends and patterns to help myself make them happen. :)

original_content: This is no longer a free app. It doesn't function as a mood tracker unless you pay $50. It'll give you a couple months free, then hold your mood results ransom. content: moodpath used really helpful tracking depressive episode evaluating severity symptom since app became minddoc though result seem much less accurate fact quite discouraging felt great week straight attestates moderate bad emotional health soon give one negative answer within week dont understand goal shift considering remove app think new negative bias could even counterproductive effect shame
original_content: Moodpath used to be really helpful in tracking depressive episodes & evaluating the severity of my symptoms. Since the app became MindDoc though, the results seem are much less accurate and are in fact quite discouraging – I felt great for weeks straight, but it attestates me moderate do bad emotional health as soon as I give one negative answer within a week. I don't understand the goal of this shift and am considering to remove the app because I think the new negative bias could even have counterproductive effects. Such a shame.

original_content: Not for Women, but better than nothing...? The app does not let you identify health markers in your profile, like biological sex. Mental health triggers, stimulus, and treatment vary wildly by sex and age, and this app doesn't track or separate that information. original_content: Pay money to become a lab rat. Moodpath pretends to be a mood tracker but is focused only on clinical depression. If you have multipolar depression or any other condition this app will give you poor results. They have virtually no support not even an email address you can contact. If you know you're depressed why are you using an app to tell you the same?

content: app great think interval time question requested editable time dont answer morning question dont pick phone right waking cant use work get answer already noon question work around fivethirtypm college pm get answer evening opinion record felt morning noon eveningnight scheduling time would awesome
original_content: The app is just great, but I think the interval of time questions are requested, should be editable. Most of the times I don't answer morning questions because I don't pick up my phone right after waking up, and can't use it at work, so when I get to answer them, it's already noon questions. And after work (around 5:30/6pm) or college (11pm) I get to answer evening's. In my opinion, it should record how I felt during my mornings, noons and evening/night, so scheduling times would be awesome!

original_content: This app is very helpful for people who want to keep track of their moodespecially if they notice that their mood has changed in a way that interferes with their life. Moodpath can generate referral letters for you, and you can connect to a therapist through the BetterHelp app or through a hotline number in case of an emergency. The only thing I wish was that you could pause monitoring for one mental health concern so that you can monitor another, or monitor two at a time.

For example, I don't sleep "well" most nights due to a medical condition that isn't mental health related. So those answers are skewed. content: using app ten day far im really impressed usually stop using mood apps quite quickly forget log mood havent missed day moodpath yet get three reminder day take one minute answer question log mood app collates response daily weekly graph look back answer see stats really helpful people mental health issue like
original_content: Been using the app for 10 days and so far I'm really impressed. I usually stop using mood apps quite quickly or forget to log my mood but I haven't missed a day with Moodpath yet. You get 3 reminders a day and it takes 1 minute to answer a few questions and log your mood. The app then collates your responses into daily and weekly graphs and you can look back at your answers and see your stats which can be really helpful for people with mental health issues like myself.

This is the only app I've been happy to pay for and feel like I'm getting so much for my money.

My issue is, some people might be confused and be relieved thinking that their problem is simply depression. A sociopath would likely receive a 'mild depression' response. It would be nice to see a system that recognizes other mental instabilities?

content: promising app take asks regular update beginning offering sensible advice training take little time interrupt day worth effort asked check seem intrusive youre trying cope crisis dont need respond every time asks longer term pay given selected reading audio exercise access professional human advice
original_content: Very promising app that takes asks for regular updates at the beginning before offering sensible advice. Training it up takes a little time, and it can interrupt your day, but it's worth the effort. Being asked to check in can seem intrusive if you're trying to cope with a crisis but you don't need to respond every time it asks, and in the longer term, it pays off. it's now given me selected reading and audio exercises, access to professional human advice

content: app simple way help learn possible mental disorder symptom may experiencing even problem seem small sign bigger issue app help track get recommended course action every fourteen day occasional mini article advice coping skill three time day answer question get write brief summary feeling also help emotional journal
original_content: This app is a very simple way to help you learn more about possible mental disorders and their symptoms that you may be experiencing. Even problems that seem small to you can be signs of bigger issues, and this app will help you track that. You get a recommended course of action every 14 days, and occasional mini articles with advice on coping skills. 3 times a day when you answer the few questions, you get to write a brief summary of your feelings so it also helps as an emotional journal.

content: basic dynamically support saying youre sleeping well doesnt give holistic picture also big factor chronic pain taking care sick kid stressful event reason sleeplessness assumes diagnosis depression need therapy based simple mental exercise despite stress another source giving anxiety need resiliency life advice overcome obstacle navigatin
original_content: It's too basic to dynamically support you. Saying that you're not sleeping well doesn't give a holistic picture because the why is also a very big factor (chronic pain, taking care of sick kids, or the stressful event was the reason for sleeplessness). It assumes a diagnosis of depression and all you need is therapy based off of simple mental exercises, despite that stress from another source is giving you anxiety, and you need resiliency or life advice to overcome obstacles, such as a navigatin

content: ive using app nearly year help manage mental health symptom great way track mood reflecting back previous month help put episode context good way reality check prepare note gp review often find type apps focusing negative emotion needed sometimes maybe recovery could longer positive reflection daily questionnaire recommend app highly
original_content: I've been using the app for nearly a year now to help manage some of my mental health symptoms. It is a great way to track moods, reflecting back on previous months helps put episodes into context. A good way to reality check and prepare notes for GP reviews. I often find these types of apps focusing on negative emotions, needed sometimes, but maybe recovery could be longer with positive reflections in the daily questionnaires. I recommend this app highly.

content: app really helped track emotionally mentally gave easy read review enabled gauge glance helped notice trend life less emotionally healthy able woro toward changing trend take ten second answer daily question set question seem adapt emotional situation pretty well would recommend mental health selfevauation tool begin processing thought
original_content: This app really helped me track how I was emotionally and mentally and gave me easy to read reviews that enabled me to gauge myself at a glance. It helped me notice trends in my life that were less than emotionally healthy so that I was able to woro toward changing those trends. It only takes 10 seconds to answer the daily question sets and the questions seem to adapt to my emotional situation pretty well. Would recommend as a mental health self-evauation tool to begin processing those thoughts.
